verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 is a mitochondrial chaperonin canonically residing inside mitochondria, but documented evidence shows it redistributes to the cell surface under defined non-baseline conditions: heat stress, inflammation, oncogenic transformation (cancer cells), and in pluripotent stem cells. Heat-stressed endothelial cells, inflammatory tissue, and tumor cells all display HSP60 on the outer leaflet. The protein undergoes active secretion in tumor cells and displays on the surface of naïve pluripotent stem cells. While the baseline extramitochondrial pool is small, stress-induced and pathological relocalization to the PM qualifies as contextual surface accessibility via cell_state_induced and tissue_restricted_surface mechanisms.
